name: AMIA Bombing
description: On 18 July 1994, a car bomb destroyed the Asociación Mutual Israelita Argentina (AMIA), a Jewish community centre in Buenos Aires, killing 85 people and injuring hundreds more. It remains the deadliest terrorist attack in Argentine history and the deadliest attack on a Jewish target since World War II. Argentine prosecutors, led by investigator Alberto Nisman, concluded that the bombing was ordered by the Iranian government and carried out by [Hezbollah](/orgs/hezbollah) operatives acting on Iranian direction. The Iranian intelligence network in Buenos Aires — operating through the [Islamic Cultural Institute of Buenos Aires](/orgs/islamic-cultural-institute-buenos-aires) — provided target intelligence and logistics. Iranian suspects identified by Argentine prosecutors include cultural attaché [Mohsen Rabbani](/people/mohsen-rabbani), Ambassador [Hadi Soleimanpour](/people/hadi-soleimanpour), and embassy third secretary [Ahmad Reza Asghari](/people/ahmad-reza-asghari). [Ali Fallahian](/people/ali-fallahian), then Minister of Intelligence, was also named. Argentina issued international arrest warrants and Interpol red notices for the Iranian suspects. In 2024, an Argentine court concluded a trial-in-absentia proceeding against several Iranian defendants.

sources.title: Blast Hits Jewish Center in Argentina, Killing at Least 48

editorial_notes: 85 killed, hundreds injured. Argentine prosecutor Alberto Nisman was found dead Jan 2015, day before planned Congressional testimony on the case.
